formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Here's the formula sheet for CBSE Class 11 Biology - Excretory Products and their Elimination in JSON format:

[
  {
    "name": "Waste products of nitrogenous metabolism",
    "formula": "Urea, uric acid, creatinine, and ammonia",
    "variables": "",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "End products of nitrogen metabolism in humans"
  },
  {
    "name": "Urea Cycle",
    "formula": "NH3 + CO2 + ATP + H2O → Urea + 2 ADP + 3 H+",
    "variables": "NH3 (ammonia), CO2 (carbon dioxide), ATP (adenosine triphosphate), H2O (water), Urea (urea), ADP (adenosine diphosphate), H+ (protons)",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Metabolic pathway that converts ammonia into urea"
  },
  {
    "name": "K+ reabsorption in the kidneys",
    "formula": "K+ reabsorption = (GFR × [K+]i) / (1 - [K+]o)",
    "variables": "K+ (potassium ions), GFR (glomerular filtration rate), [K+]i (potassium ions in the blood), [K+]o (potassium ions in the filtrate)",
    "units": "mmol/min",
    "notes": "Equation for the reabsorption of potassium ions in the kidneys"
  },
  {
    "name": "pH regulation in the kidneys",
    "formula": "pH = [H+] × 10^(-pOH)",
    "variables": "pH (acidity), [H+] (hydrogen ions), pOH (basicity)",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Equation for calculating pH from hydrogen ion concentration"
  },
  {
    "name": "Osmoregulation in the kidneys",
    "formula": "ΔP = Δπ + Δσ",
    "variables": "ΔP (total filtration pressure), Δπ (osmotic pressure), Δσ (hydrostatic pressure)",
    "units": "mmHg",
    "notes": "Equation for the net filtration pressure in the kidneys"
  },
  {
    "name": "K+ secretion in the kidneys",
    "formula": "K+ secretion = (K+ in the filtrate) - (K+ reabsorbed)",
    "variables": "K+ (potassium ions), K+ in the filtrate (potassium ions in the filtrate), K+ reabsorbed (potassium ions reabsorbed)",
    "units": "mmol/min",
    "notes": "Equation for potassium ion secretion in the kidneys"
  },
  {
    "name": "Renal clearance",
    "formula": "C = V × (Sf / Pf)",
    "variables": "C (renal clearance), V (volume of plasma), Sf (solute concentration in the filtrate), Pf (solute concentration in the plasma)",
    "units": "mL/min",
    "notes": "Equation for calculating renal clearance"
  },
  {
    "name": "Glomerular filtration rate (GFR)",
    "formula": "GFR = (Kf × Sf) / (1 - (H2O in the filtrate) / (H2O in the plasma))",
    "variables": "GFR (glomerular filtration rate), Kf (filtration coefficient), Sf (filtrate flow rate), H2O in the filtrate (water in the filtrate), H2O in the plasma (water in the plasma)",
    "units": "mL/min",
    "notes": "Equation for calculating glomerular filtration rate"
  },
  {
    "name": "Blood urea nitrogen (BUN)",
    "formula": "BUN = (Urea in the blood) / (Creatinine in the blood)",
    "variables": "BUN (blood urea nitrogen), Urea in the blood (urea concentration in the blood), Creatinine in the blood (creatinine concentration in the blood)",
    "units": "mg/dL",
    "notes": "Equation for calculating blood urea nitrogen"
  },
  {
    "name": "Creatinine clearance",
    "formula": "Creatinine clearance = (Creatinine in the urine) / (Creatinine in the blood)",
    "variables": "Creatinine clearance (creatine clearance), Creatinine in the urine (creatinine in the urine), Creatinine in the blood (creatinine in the blood)",
    "units": "mL/min",
    "notes": "Equation for calculating creatinine clearance"
  }
]

Note: Some of the formulas may not have direct mathematical expressions, but they represent key concepts in the chapter.
